changes.before: Horses waited under the ward hum, dark at the knees with road mud, while another runestone answered deeper in the trees.
changes.after: Trimmed the horse-detail tail and left the sweep plus ward answer.

changes.before: ...the part of Quel'Thalas that made sense: bow across her back, road under hoof, a border that could be ridden and held by living hands instead of argued over under painted ceilings in Silvermoon.
changes.after: ...the part of Quel'Thalas that made sense: road under hoof, a border held by living hands instead of argued over in Silvermoon.

changes.before: Sylvanas kept count because she tracked what guarded her lands...
changes.after: Sylvanas kept count: gaps in birdsong, iron against leather, the hum deepening at another node in the ring.

changes.before: The words came clipped by training and short by breath.
changes.after: Kept the runner strained but cut the repeated breath scaffolding around the report.

changes.before: A human mage inside the ward network, talking about leylines at dawn, meant one of those and a problem with her stones besides.
changes.after: Inside the ward network, talking about leylines at dawn, meant a problem with her stones besides.

changes.before: That got the breath of a laugh she wanted.
changes.after: That got the laugh she wanted.

changes.before: ...she took two rangers to the second marker, finding the prisoner...
changes.after: ...found the prisoner at the second marker...

changes.before: She held onto that. Court mages came polished...
changes.after: Cut the filter sentence and let the contrast carry the inference.

changes.before: Stone buildings against the slope, watch platforms in the trees, a lean-to stable, three ward monoliths...
changes.after: Stone buildings against the slope, watch platforms in the trees, three ward monoliths...

changes.before: When I told her she could answer questions after she sat down instead of reaching for the staff, she said I was wasting time we did not have.
changes.after: ...asked whether we'd felt the bad patch on the north sweep, and said we were wasting time.

changes.before: No ornamental nonsense. No diplomatic letters tucked between the pages in case manners failed.
changes.after: No diplomatic letters tucked between the pages in case manners failed.

changes.before: Stone box at the edge of the yard, built for smugglers, spies, and the occasional fool who mistook elven borders for suggestions...
changes.after: Stone box at the edge of the yard: narrow window, iron-bound door, blue ward-light caged along the threshold.

changes.before: blue light climbing Sylvanas's boots and sharpening the iron bands while inside one person breathed too tightly to pass for calm
changes.after: blue light climbing Sylvanas's boots while inside one person breathed too tightly to pass for calm

changes.before: The prisoner stood too fast for indifference and too controlled for panic, in travel robes with a dusty hem and boots grey with miles. Bare hands, one finger stained with ink.
changes.after: The prisoner stood too fast for indifference and too controlled for panic, in travel robes with a dusty hem and boots grey with miles, one finger stained with ink.

changes.before: Hair the color of late wheat had more weather than care in it now. Younger than the dossier suggested. Not soft.
changes.after: Hair the color of late wheat had more weather than care in it. Younger than the dossier suggested. Not soft.

observations.note: Polish input came from v4b.md, not v4.md.
POV remains Sylvanas limited-third; trims kept tactical noticing over emotional labeling.
Story arc check confirmed chapter sits in the witty procedural phase, so the dry Lor'themar banter stayed.
Voice-sheet check: Sylvanas still uses finished judgments and command-shaped questions.
Voice-sheet check: Jaina's entrance stays precise and work-worn rather than modern-technocratic.
Word count dropped 3204→2563, a reduction of 641 words (~20.0%).
Opening patrol section lost decorative horse and chamber detail but kept the patrol-road-vs-court contrast.
Runner briefing was compressed in two places so urgency lands faster.
Outpost arrival lost one inventory item and one explanatory beat without changing the tactical picture.
Holding-cell entry kept the accusation pressure but shed architecture and inventory padding.
Narrator-over-explanation was the main remaining problem; multiple sentences restated points the concrete detail already carried.
The line about Quel'Thalas “making sense” originally overexplained the road-vs-court contrast; the trimmed version trusts the image.
Sylvanas's counting paragraph now lands directly in sensory/tactical terms instead of glossing her habit.
The “rot” reflection was tightened so it reads as judgment, not a mini essay.
Jaina's satchel description had inventory bloat; the revision keeps the evidence that matters: notes, fieldwork, route spread.
The holding-room exterior originally explained the building too much; the revision keeps only the pieces Sylvanas would clock at speed.
Jaina's first visual pass lost one redundant sentence so the split knuckles carry more weight.
No document-voice offenders such as actionable/vector/optimal/diagnostic remained in narration or dialogue.
Dialogue register was already clean; no spoken line slipped into bureaucratic or lab-report diction.
Lor'themar's humor stayed smooth and aristocratic rather than flippant.
Runner dialogue stayed plain and field-practical; no false simplification prompt/reply issue was present.
Cyndia's dialogue still reads like a competent ranger, not an exposition device.
Jaina is introduced through others' reports and physical evidence, so her actual spoken register remains untouched for the chapter-end reveal.
Meta-audit on the densest late paragraphs found no new eye/gaze tic introduced by the trims.
Breath-reaction count fell from 3 to 0 in the final draft.
Filter-word count stayed low; one remaining “felt” belongs to the runner's report rather than narrator distance.
The final line still opens the interrogation cleanly and preserves the chapter-turn hook.
